agency: New York City Department of Sanitation (DSNY)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting; robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Homepage + section pages fingerprinted from headers; the /assets/dsny/forms/ React SPA bundle (index-*.js) reverse-engineered to recover the undocumented first-party backend APIs. Socrata catalog API queried for the DSNY agency footprint (Dataset-Information_Agency = 'Department of Sanitation (DSNY)', verified).
headline_findings: DSNY already runs REAL first-party backend APIs — but every one is undocumented and reachable only by reverse-engineering the DSNY React forms: dsnypublic.nyc.gov/dsny/api (geocoder/DSNYCollection collection-schedule lookup, geocoder/BCW), the ePickups API (ePickupsAPI/api/PickupRequest/AddUpdatePickUpRequest, GetUnavailableDates, IsDistrictActive), and the CFC appointment API (dsnydonate.nyc.gov/cfc/api/appointment).

So the net-new WRITE object — BulkPickupRequest (schedule a bulk/appointment pickup) — is not truly net-new capability; the ePickups backend already does it. What is net-new is an OWNED, documented, agent-native contract over it.

51 DSNY datasets on NYC Open Data (356K lifetime views), led by DSNY Monthly Tonnage (ebb7-mvp5, 58K), Public Recycling Bins, Recycling Diversion & Capture Rates, and Electronics/Food-Scrap/Special-Waste drop-off locations.

The website is Oracle WebCenter Sites (.page URLs, livesite-version header) with React SPA forms; backends are ASP.NET Web API on IIS behind ARR, with permissive CORS (access-control-allow-origin: *).
See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md for the vendor + API inventory.

apis_observed.note: First-party. Resolves an address to a DSNY district + collection schedule; BCW geocodes commercial-waste zones. No docs, no OpenAPI.
First-party ASP.NET Web API. Already schedules bulk/large-item pickups (AppointmentDate/Time/Items, BulkPickup). CORS *. The backend behind the net-new BulkPickupRequest.
First-party CFC/special-waste drop-off appointment booking behind the React forms.
ASP.NET Web API; permissive CORS; serves the DSNY forms/services.
Open JSON content API (200) serving DSNY page/form content.
51 DSNY datasets (tonnage, recycling rates, drop-off sites, litter baskets, districts/zones).
Where missed-collection and sanitation complaints are filed today.

fruit.name: Collection schedule lookup
fruit.notes: A React form calls dsnypublic.nyc.gov/dsny/api/geocoder/DSNYCollection to return refuse/recycling/organics days. Real first-party API, but undocumented and forms-only.

fruit.name: DSNY districts / sections / zones + frequencies
fruit.notes: Operational geography and per-stream collection frequencies (refuse/recycling/organics/bulk/e-waste). The join key the schedule geocoder resolves to.

fruit.name: E-waste / food-scrap / special-waste drop-off sites
fruit.notes: Several point datasets across material types; special-waste (CFC) sites book via dsnydonate.nyc.gov/cfc/api/appointment. Unified here as one DropOffSite resource.

fruit.name: Litter basket inventory
fruit.notes: Every corner litter basket citywide with type, owner, and location — a rich machine-readable dataset with no resource API.

fruit.name: Monthly tonnage & recycling rates
opendata_match: Recycling Diversion and Capture Rates gaq9-z3hz (9c)
fruit.notes: The most-viewed DSNY dataset (58K views). Refuse/paper/MGP/organics tons per community district per month; recycling rates alongside.

fruit.name: Public recycling bins
fruit.notes: On-street public recycling bins (paper + MGP) with partner and DSNY zone/district. Modeled as a DropOffSite material type.

fruit.name: Commercial waste zones + rate calculator
fruit.notes: The commercial-waste franchise zones; a rate-calculator form uses the geocoder/BCW backend + ArcGIS layers.

fruit.name: Bulk / large-item / special-waste pickup request
fruit.notes: NET-NEW owned contract. The ePickups backend (ePickupsAPI/api/PickupRequest/AddUpdatePickUpRequest, GetUnavailableDates, IsDistrictActive) already schedules pickups, but there is no documented, agent-native API for it. The one transactional write surface promoted to a first-class resource.

fruit.name: Missed-collection complaint
fruit.notes: Missed-collection and sanitation complaints route through NYC 311, not a DSNY API — outside the DSNY-owned surface. Noted, not modeled here.

fruit.name: Sanitation enforcement / violations
fruit.notes: Illegal-dumping, street-vending, and dirty-sidewalk enforcement is described on-site; the summons/violation records live in OATH/ECB, not a DSNY dataset — a gap.

fruit.name: Waste Equity Law Data CY2019
fruit.notes: Local Law 152 waste-equity data by community district, CY2019.

fruit.name: Waste Equity Law Data CY2020
fruit.notes: Waste-equity capacity and tonnage by community district, CY2020.

fruit.notes: Waste-equity capacity and tonnage by community district, CY2021-2022.

fruit.notes: Waste-equity capacity and tonnage by community district, CY2023.

fruit.notes: Waste-equity capacity and tonnage by community district, CY2024.

fruit.notes: Fiscal-year-to-date curbside collection and diversion tonnage by district/stream.

fruit.notes: Pesticide application log at the Southwest Brooklyn Marine Transfer Station.
